news.title: Clock is ticking: Flores says House impeachment proceedings are 'time-bound'
news.description: A vice chairman of the House Committee on Justice has highlighted the "time-bound" nature of the impeachment proceedings against Vice President Sara Duterte. This means it is incumbent upon the committee to see the proceedings through, despite the notion that it should stop in deference to the national energy emergency, says Bukidnon 2nd district Rep. Jonathan Keith Flores. Flores, in a recent online interview, rejected calls to suspend the impeachment hearings amid the energy crisis linked to tensions in the Middle East. He said the Constitution itself imposed strict timelines on the House. He says the chamber is time-bound in acting on impeachment cases, noting that the Supreme Court (SC) has previously ruled against the House for violating such timelines in the 2025 impeachment proceedings against Duterte. “Kaya ngayon (Even now), if we say na, can we just set this aside because we’re in a crisis. We can’t, eh. Because the Constitution mandates that we have to deal with it,” Flores said. The 2026 impeachment proceedings against the Vice President were formally initiated by the House of Representatives last Feb. 23. From there, the justice panel has 60 session days to determine the sufficiency in form, substance, and grounds of the filed complaints. Within that period, the committee must be able to endorse its findings to the plenary, which would then vote whether or not to impeach Duterte and put her on trial before the Senate impeachment court. Flores has also noted that only around 50 congressmen are currently involved in the justice committee hearings. “There are 300 plus members [in the House of Representatives]. We’re just 50 something in the Committee on Justice and we just meet once a week lang, ‘di ba (right)? We do other things po other than just the impeachment. Ang dami po naming ginagawa (We\'re doing a lot of things),” Flores said. In the same interview, Flores, a Mindanaonon, acknowledged the pushback in his home region as far as Duterte\'s impeachment was concerned. After all, the Vice President is from Davao City. “It’s a very difficult position to be in,” the Bukidnon congressman said. Last year, Flores was picked to be part of the prosecution panel against Duterte, although the actual Senate impeachment trial didn\'t push through. Now, he continues to play a big role in the proceedings. “It’s not like I want to. It’s not like I volunteered to do it but it’s part of my duty as a member, as a vice chairperson of the Committee on Justice,” he said. “It’s as if wala namang choice (there is no choice), it’s always vested in the House, the initiation of the impeachment. So, kung nandun ka (So if you\'re there), you have to be part of it. You cannot just run away from it.”

news.title: DOJ, Ombudsman ‘overwhelmed’ with ICI cases — Palace
news.description: Good evening. You’re watching The Manila Times’ News Prime. Today is April 1st. Our top story: The Justice Department (DOJ) and the Office of the Ombudsman are “overwhelmed” by the volume of cases referred by the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI). Palace Press Officer Claire Castro issued the statement after several groups expressed dissatisfaction with the ICI’s investigation into flood control projects. The ICI concluded its operations yesterday. It has already submitted its findings to proper authorities. The findings include referrals involving dozens of persons linked to questionable infrastructure projects. Castro says the ICI is just a fact-finding committee. According to Castro, the ICI members are not the ones who will hold accountable the so-called “big fish.” She stressed the investigating bodies are the DOJ and the Ombudsman. Castro said the volume of referrals has placed pressure on investigating bodies, which were now conducting preliminary investigations. Read more: https://tmt.news/2312196 Public trust and approval ratings for the country’s top leaders declined further in the first quarter of 2026. That’s according to the latest Pahayag survey by Publicus Asia Inc. The nationwide poll was conducted from March 21 to 24. The respondents were over 1,000 registered voters. President Marcos’ approval rating dropped to 19 percent, down from 22 percent in the previous quarter. His trust rating fell to 13 percent. Respondents cited three main issues behind the decline: The issues are the perceived failure to ensure accountability in flood control controversies, backlash over developments involving former President Rodrigo Duterte and the International Criminal Court, and dissatisfaction with rising fuel prices. Vice President Sara Duterte remains the highest-rated among top officials. But she also posted significant declines. Her approval rating fell to 28 percent from 34 percent. Her trust rating dropped to 26 percent from 31 percent. Her numbers’ decline was due to the impeachment complaints against her, political tensions, and developments linked to the ICC case involving her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte. Read more: https://tmt.news/2312298 The MMDA and the Metro Pacific Tollways Corporation are reminding Holy Week motorists to inspect their vehicles before heading out. They also advised travelers to keep emergency and roadside assistance hotlines ready. Authorities are bracing for heavier movement on major roads and expressways in the next few days. The “Biyaheng Arangkada” motorist assistance program has been rolled out in various expressways across the country. Medical teams are on standby at select stops. Free round-the-clock basic mechanical services are also available at all NLEX and SCTEX rest and refuel stations. Free towing to the nearest exit for Class 1 vehicles would be available until 6 a.m. on April 6. Motorists needing traffic assistance in Metro Manila may call the MMDA hotline 136. For urgent emergencies such as crashes or incidents needing immediate response, travelers may call the national emergency hotline 911. Read more: https://tmt.news/2312288 The LTFRB has approved a 10 percent fare discount for passengers traveling on select buses nationwide. The discount will be from Good Friday to Easter Sunday. It covers the operations of the Yanson Group of Bus Companies. LTFRB Chairman Vigor Mendoza says the fare reduction will apply across the group’s major bus operators serving routes in Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao. The Yanson Group says the temporary fare cut is part of the national government’s call for “bayanihan” during the Holy Week travel period. Metro Manila mall operators have announced adjusted operating hours for Holy Week. Most of them will be closed on Maundy Thursday and Good Friday. However, select locations and essential services like supermarkets will remain accessible to the public. SM Supermalls will close most of its Metro Manila branches on Maundy Thursday and Good Friday. SM Mall of Asia, SM Megamall, SM North EDSA, and SM City Manila are closed on April 2 to 3. They will be open on April 4 to 5 from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m. SM Aura Premier will be closed April 2 to 3. However, its supermarket will be open from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. on Thursday and 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. on Friday. It reopens for regular hours on April 4. For the Holy Week operating hours of other malls, you may visit The Manila Times website. Read more: https://tmt.news/2312166 For more news and information, read The Manila Times on print, subscribe to its digital edition or log on to www.manilatimes.net. Follow us on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, TikTok, WhatsApp, and LinkedIn; and be part of our communities on Viber, Telegram, and Mastodon. That’s all for now on The Manila Times’ News Prime. Remember to stay safe, stay informed, and stay connected. I’m Michael Ang. Good night.

news.title: Duterte impeachment: Public should monitor ‘overdue’ hearings — Cendaña
news.description: MANILA, Philippines — Akbayan party-list Rep. Percival Cendaña has urged the public to monitor the upcoming House hearings on the impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte, saying the proceedings have been “long overdue” due to the respondent’s repeated evasion of accountability measures. In an interview on Wednesday, Cendaña said they expect Ramil Madriaga, Duterte’s former aide, to deliver his testimony on April 14. “We are also expecting that on the 14th, the people will intently watch and listen, as this has been long overdue. They have been waiting for evidence and the truth to come out for a long time [...]... Keep on reading: Duterte impeachment: Public should monitor ‘overdue’ hearings — Cendaña

news.title: As Iran–Israel war rages, are Tel Aviv’s underground hospitals turning into infection hotspots?
news.description: The purpose of these hospitals were to shield sick Israeli citizens from missiles and drones, the conditions inside these compounds are anything but ideal to treat them. These compounds have started witnessing other problems: overcrowding, erosion of medical staff, and an increased risk of contracting diseases and infections.
